rationale: At pinned SHA 77d8a1a9, all 12 requested named tests are absent (each existence grep exits 1), the AC4 ScanOrphanedClosedBeads anchor is absent from internal code, and every full AC1-AC7 proof chain exits 1. AC7 is correctly split without a cross-package cycle; branchless recovery names branch-ref restoration; h4n5 occurs exactly once; and AC10 enumerates all ownership files and resolves solely to workflow. One strict runnability requirement remains unmet: AC1 existence-checks TestScanOrphanedClosedBeads_ErrorPreserving but follows it with package-wide `go test ./internal/lifecycle`, not the required exact-named `go test -run 'TestScanOrphanedClosedBeads_ErrorPreserving'`. The validation-proofs claim that every named test has an exact-named run is therefore false. This is a small proof correction, but the panel brief makes it approval-blocking.
concrete_changes_required: Amend AC1 so the TestScanOrphanedClosedBeads_ErrorPreserving existence grep is chained to `go test ./internal/lifecycle -run 'TestScanOrphanedClosedBeads_ErrorPreserving' -v`; retain an additional full-package lifecycle test if desired.

findings.note: The spec adds BranchExistsE, makes branch probing the fourth error-preserving fail-closed leg, preserves the swallowing wrapper, adds the named approve fault test, and scopes internal/gitutil to execution.
All requested existence greps and all AC1-AC7 proof chains are RED at 77d8a1a9, but AC1 does not exact-run the named lifecycle test after checking its existence.
AC7 is split into package-local complete and approve tests, with separate existence checks and exact-named runs; current source imports show no reverse package edge.
The branchless obligation refusal has distinct recourse requiring restoration of bead/<id> before mindspec complete, and AC6 explicitly asserts that prerequisite.
grep -c mindspec-h4n5 .beads/issues.jsonl returns exactly 1 at the pinned SHA.
AC10 greps the complete .mindspec/domains/*/OWNERSHIP.yaml set and asserts the sole output is workflow; the proof passes at the pinned SHA.
cmd/** is dropped as a change surface, and the execution self-ownership gap is explicitly dispositioned as pre-existing and out of scope.
